creator: cem@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Eugene Grosbein from comment #13)
(In reply to Shreesh Holla from comment #14)
Yeah, just bumping i386 KSTACK_PAGES will at least give parity with amd64.  That's reasonable.  It seems kind of absurd we knowingly ship a broken ZFS on i386 (see UPDATING's reference to KSTACK_PAGES).

If people are concerned about a KSTACK_PAGES=2 architecture "keeping us honest," they are free to set KSTACK_PAGES=2 on their own amd64 systems' configuration, rather than inflicting the pain upon i386 users.
